sections.paragraph:
λεῖμμα, ατος, τό (s. λείπω; edd. also λείμμα, λίμμα; Hdt. 1, 119 et al.; Plut., Mor. 78a.—PTebt 115, 2 [115/113 b.c.] τὸ γεγονὼς [=-ὸς] λίμμα [Mayser p. 843]; 4 Km 19:4 A λίμματος [Thackeray p. 84]; W-S. §5; 13e; New Docs 4, 186) remnant λ. κατʼ ἐκλογὴν χάριτος γέγονεν there is a remnant chosen by grace Ro 11:5 (cp. CD 1, 4).—JJeremias, D. Gedanke des ‘Heiligen Restes’ usw., ZNW 42, ’49, 154–94.—DELG s.v. λείπω. M-M. TW.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

CD CD = Cairo (Genizah text of the) Damascus (Document), a.k.a. Zadokite Document or Damascus Rule—List 2, end

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 590
